title: Lemon Cream Cheese Squares
ingredients: 8 tbsp unsalted butter, room temperature, cut into 8 pieces
10 ounces Philadelphia cream cheese, at room temperature
½ cup plus 1 tbsp granulated sugar
¼ cup plus 1 tbsp sour cream, room temperature
instructions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees F and lightly grease an 8-inch square pan with butter.
Process flour and sugar in a food processor for 20 seconds.
Add the butter and process until dough comes together, about 20-30 seconds.
Pat the dough gently over the bottom of the prepared pan.
Glaze the dough with egg white by pouring it on the dough and tipping the pan from side to side so it spreads over the surface, then pour off the excess.
Bake the base on the center oven rack until golden, about 25 minutes.
Place the base in the refrigerator for 15 minutes to cool while keeping the oven on.
While the base is cooling, place cream cheese, granulated sugar, and lemon zest in a medium-sized bowl and beat until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes, scraping the sides of the bowl a couple times.
Add the eggs and vanilla and beat on medium-high heat until smooth and creamy, about 10 seconds.
Spread the lemon cheesecake topping evenly over the cooled base.
Bake the bars on the center oven rack until the top is slightly golden, about 1 hour, pricking any bubbles that form with a toothpick.
While the lemon cream cheese layer is baking, combine the sour cream layer ingredients (2 cups sour cream, ½ cup sugar, and 1 tsp vanilla) in a medium bowl and let sit to allow sugar to dissolve.
Remove the baked lemon cream cheese layer from the oven and let cool for 15 minutes.
Pour the sour cream mixture on top and return to the oven for 10 more minutes.
Remove from the oven and allow to cool completely, then refrigerate.
Cut into bars using a sharp knife, wiping the blade between cuts.
notes: All dairy ingredients (cream cheese, sour cream, eggs) should be at room temperature for best results.
The lemon cheesecake layer may bubble during baking; use a toothpick to gently prick any bubbles.
Wiping the knife between cuts helps achieve clean, even bars.
The recipe requires three distinct layers: buttery flour base, lemon cheesecake filling, and sour cream topping.
